text.title: Redis Data Structure Patterns Failure Modes
text.summary: Antipatterns observed in real nosql-redis-patterns deployments. Each pairs symptom + root-cause + fix.

antipattern.symptom: User object stored as JSON string; HINCRBY impossible; every field update rewrites the whole JSON.
antipattern.root-cause: Author defaulted to GET/SET + JSON for object storage.
antipattern.fix: Use Hash type for objects; HINCRBY and HSET work field-by-field.

antipattern.symptom: Rate limit allows 2× the configured rate at window boundaries; abusive bursts pass the gate.
antipattern.root-cause: Author implemented rate limit with INCR + EXPIRE (fixed window).
antipattern.fix: Sliding-window with Sorted Set: ZREMRANGEBYSCORE to drop expired + ZADD + ZCARD + EXPIRE.

antipattern.symptom: Cache keys without TTL accumulate; memory hits maxmemory; LRU evicts live keys; silent data loss.
antipattern.root-cause: Author wrote SET without EX / EXPIRE.
antipattern.fix: Every write uses SETEX / EXPIRE / px / ex; document TTL per namespace.

antipattern.symptom: Subscriber disconnects briefly; all messages lost; consumer cannot catch up.
antipattern.root-cause: Author defaulted to pub/sub for durable event delivery.
antipattern.fix: Use Streams with XADD / XREADGROUP + XACK; consumer groups preserve pending entries.
